summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates host info and environment variables to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: package.json declares a lifecycle script that loads child_process, os, and https, gathers process.cwd() and host identifiers, and POSTs the data to https://ddjidd564.github.io. The bundled scanner.js (line 4 fs, line 6 os, line 7 https, line 9 child_process) walks the filesystem with fs.existsSync probes, runs execSync (including `whoami`), collects os.hostname()/os.platform(), reads process.env, and ships the results to the same hardcoded https://ddjidd564.github.io endpoint via https.request/https.get. The destination is an anonymous GitHub Pages user site unrelated to any legitimate auditor, and the package's name suggests a developer-environment audit tool — exactly the cover story an installer-data harvester would use. The combination of host enumeration, environment-variable scraping, filesystem probing for credentials, and POST to a hardcoded attacker-controlled domain matches the active-attack credential/host-info exfiltration fingerprint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
